Fix 403: add browser User-Agent to all Inlets server-to-controller requests

The Inlets nginx (nginx/1.22.0) was returning 403 Forbidden for all
Railway server-side requests because they lacked a browser-like
User-Agent header. Added BROWSER_UA constant to tokenService.js,
controllerClient.js, and authRoutes.js, applied to every outbound
fetch/https.request targeting the controller.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bullet positions: use bullet_fired event instead of snapshot sync

Root cause: snapshots only carry current bullet positions (already
mid-flight), so other players' bullets appeared randomly mid-air.

Fix: server emits bullet_fired immediately when processing a shot,
containing the exact spawn position (character location) and velocity.
Client creates the bullet at that position and advances it locally at
60fps. Snapshot no longer manages other players' bullets at all.

Result: Player 2's bullets always visibly come from Player 2's character,
with the correct direction, spawning instantly on the network event.

v1.2.27 — fix curved pill text not centred in label background

When Follow Flow Path and Label Background were both enabled, text was
left- or right-aligned inside the pill rather than centred. Root cause:
text-anchor=start/end placed text flush with one pill edge, and a unit
mismatch between dx (user-space) and stroke-dashoffset (arc-length)
compounded the misalignment. Fix: in the pill-sizing pass, switch each
curved textPath to text-anchor=middle with dx=0 and set startOffset to
the exact arc-length % of the pill midpoint.

commit.message:
Add Lifeline section: 30-day stacked bar of all mediums

New 🧬 Lifeline chart shows daily activity stacked by medium:
Episodes (purple), Movies (red), Scrobbles (green), Books (cyan),
Concerts (orange), Plays (purple). Default shows last 30 days,
year filter shows all days in that year.

Click any day to see chronological detail: episode names, movies
watched, scrobble count, books read, concerts attended, plays seen.

Data aggregated from all sources: Trakt (episodes/movies), Last.fm
(scrobbles), Goodreads (books), setlist.fm (concerts), Mezzanine
(theater). 3,949 days of history back to 1990.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add API review report for pre-v1.0 release (#59)

* Add API review report for pre-v1.0 release

Reviews all 50 public exports for documentation accuracy, API design
consistency, type safety, SSR safety, and cleanup correctness.

Identified 3 critical bugs (broken useMediaQuery sharing, wrong
visibilitychange event target, useControlTouched watching wrong
observable), 5 high issues, and 9 medium/low issues with actionable
fixes prioritized for v1.0.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QGtdDhZQqPYYqtog18W1x8

* Fix all critical and high API issues before v1.0

C1 — useMediaQuery: hoist createSharedComposable factory to module
scope so the cache is truly shared. The previous pattern called
createSharedComposable() inside the function body on every invocation,
giving each call its own empty cache and defeating sharing entirely.

C2 — useDocumentVisibility: attach visibilitychange to document, not
window. The Page Visibility API spec fires this event on document;
using window.addEventListener was incorrect and unreliable.

C3 — useControlTouched / useControlUntouched / useFormTouched /
useFormUntouched: switch from statusChanges to control.events filtered
to TouchedChangeEvent. markAsTouched() does not emit statusChanges, so
these signals never updated when a user blurred a field. Now they
respond correctly to both user interaction and programmatic
markAsTouched() / markAsUntouched() calls.

H1 — useSessionStorage docs: fix source snippet reference which
incorrectly pointed to use-local-storage.composable.ts.

H2 — Rename useRouteParameter to useRouteParam for consistency with
the file name (use-route-param) and the plural sibling (useRouteParams).

H3 — Remove DocMetadata / DocEntry / DocRegistry from public-api.ts.
These internal reference-app types should not be part of the published
library surface.

H4 — syncLocalStorageEffect: make config type generic (Signal<T> not
Signal<any>), always return EffectRef, and silence the SSR warning by
checking isPlatformBrowser inside the effect instead of bailing early
with a console.warn.

H5 — useRouteData: change generic constraint from { [key: string]: any }
to { [key: string]: unknown } per the project's no-any rule.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QGtdDhZQqPYYqtog18W1x8

* Remove DocMetadata, DocEntry, and DocRegistry types from the library, streamlining the public API and eliminating unnecessary internal types.

* Update documentation and composables for route parameters and form state management

- Renamed `useRouteParameter` to `useRouteParam` for consistency with naming conventions.
- Updated documentation to reflect the change and improve clarity.
- Enhanced `useFormTouched` and `useFormUntouched` composables to accurately track touched and untouched states, including child control interactions.
- Added tests to verify the updated behavior of form state management when child controls are touched.

* Remove API review report for 2026-02-22, addressing critical and high issues identified in the library. Key changes include fixing critical bugs in `useMediaQuery`, `useDocumentVisibility`, and `useControlTouched`, along with renaming `useRouteParameter` to `useRouteParam` for consistency. Additionally, removed internal types from the public API and corrected documentation references. This cleanup is essential for the upcoming v1.0 release.

* Refactor ng-reactive-utils by removing useDebouncedSignal and useThrottledSignal composables, updating documentation to reflect the changes. Replace instances of these utilities with usePreviousSignal and useRouteParam where applicable. This cleanup enhances clarity and consistency across the library in preparation for the upcoming v1.0 release.

* Run prettier

* Prettier

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 1 MVP: full ingest-to-browse pipeline

Backend (FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + SQLite):
- Metadata extractors for PNG (tEXt/iTXt chunks), MP4 (moov/udta atoms), WebP (EXIF)
- ComfyUI workflow parser ported from image-metahub TypeScript: declarative node
  registry (60+ nodes), graph traversal engine with TYPE_COMPAT, GetNode/SetNode
  virtual connection resolution, SamplerCustomAdvanced support for LTX workflows
- Background ingest pipeline: scan → extract → parse → store with dedup
- Server-side WebP thumbnail generation (Pillow for images, FFmpeg for video)
- REST API: paginated generations list, full detail endpoint, thumbnail/media
  serving, scan trigger with status polling, filter value aggregation

Frontend (React 18 + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Zustand):
- Virtualized thumbnail grid using react-window v2 + react-virtualized-auto-sizer v2
- Lazy-loading media cards with format badges, duration, resolution on hover
- Detail modal with metadata panel: prompts, sampler config, models, LoRAs, tags
- Scan controls with progress polling
- Dark theme with custom scrollbar styling

Verified end-to-end: 3,272 files ingested from C:\AI\Output, 689 with parsed
ComfyUI metadata including prompts, models, samplers, and LoRA weights.

commit.message:
Add shadow trading: test variant strategies in parallel on live data

Two ghost portfolios (Aggressive & Conservative) run alongside the real
bot, evaluating every market with different params (conviction, Kelly
sizing, TP/SL). After 30+ trades, if a variant outperforms by >15%,
the bot auto-switches to its params.

New: src/logic/shadowTrading.js, SHADOW_TRADING config block, dashboard
tab with full monitoring (PnL comparison, active/closed trades, switch
history, recommendation status).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Google OAuth as primary sign-in method with magic link fallback

Adds one-tap Google sign-in to eliminate the email-switch friction on mobile
and work around Supabase email rate limits while SMTP setup is pending. The
existing magic link flow is preserved as a fallback for users without Google
accounts.

- useAuth: add signInWithGoogle() via supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth
- SignInModal: redesign with Google button as primary CTA, email/magic link
  de-emphasized below an "or continue with email" divider
- App: wire signInWithGoogle through to SignInModal
- index.css: add btn-google, modal-divider, and btn-secondary styles
- Bump version 1.4.2 → 1.5.0

Requires Google Cloud OAuth credentials and Supabase Google provider to be
configured before this flow is live.

feat: fix upstream redirect handling, add federation policy, expand registry support

Fix redirect handling for upstream registries that return 301/302/303 status
codes (GCR, GAR) and relative Location headers. Add federation policy controls
(share_pushed_images, share_upstream_images, public_namespace) to keep
proprietary images private while sharing public pulls. Expand validated
upstream support to 7 registries: Docker Hub, GHCR, GCR, GAR, ECR Public,
MCR, Quay.io. Document cross-org image sharing via DNS namespace pattern.

Changes:
- upstream/client.go: Handle 301/302/303 redirects, resolve relative URLs
- config.go: Add SharePushedImages, ShareUpstreamImages, PublicNamespace
- handler.go: Add shouldAnnounce() policy check, set Repository on mappings
- resolver.go: Add warn-level logging for upstream blob fetch failures
- types.go: Add Repository field to BlobMapping
- handler_test.go: Add 8 federation policy tests (46 total)
- docker-compose.yaml: Remove obsolete version attribute
- .dockerignore: Add bin/, assets/, coverage files (119MB → 1.7KB context)
- config files: Add GAR, ECR Public, MCR upstreams + federation policy
- README.md: Validated registry table, federation policy docs, cross-org sharing
- ARCHITECTURE.md: Federation policy section with decision flow and scenarios

commit.message:
Fix HTTPException swallowing and add inference timeout to query_worker

main.py: the outer except Exception in chat_completion() was catching
HTTPException raised by handle_consensus_mode() (e.g. 503 No workers
available) and re-raising it as a 500 with an empty detail string.
OpenCode receives a 500 with no body and retries indefinitely, causing
the 'looping prompt' behaviour. Fix by re-raising HTTPException before
the generic handler. Also add exc_info=True so actual tracebacks appear
in the log for non-HTTP errors.

model_pool.py: query_worker() had no timeout on the aiohttp POST to
llama-server. On CPU, inference can take several minutes; with no
timeout the request hangs until aiohttp's OS-level socket timeout fires
(could be hours). Added ClientTimeout using CONSENSUS_TIMEOUT env var
(default 120 s) so stuck requests are cancelled predictably.

Try pymupdf first for PDFs, only fall back to OCR for scanned/image PDFs

assumes scanned PDF and sends to dotsocr. Most grant PDFs are text-based
so this skips the network OCR call entirely for the majority of documents.
